message:
test(fuzz): seed fuzz_sentinel_parsers corpus + lock parser contract

Add a 12-seed fuzz corpus for the sentinel parser surface and
pin the contract for it inside fr-sentinel's unit tests, in the
same vein as the existing `fuzz_config_file`, `fuzz_dump_restore`,
and `fuzz_acl_rules` corpus + contract pairings landed earlier on
main.

Corpus (`fuzz/corpus/fuzz_sentinel_parsers/`):

HELLO message seeds — drive `HelloMessage::parse`:
- `hello_valid_ipv4.txt` — canonical PUBLISH-style HELLO from a
  sentinel at 26379 announcing master at 6379.
- `hello_valid_hostname.txt` — same shape but with a hostname,
  not an IP, in the master-address position.
- `hello_invalid_port.txt` — non-numeric port to hit the
  parse-int branch.
- `hello_invalid_missing_fields.txt` — short field count, drives
  the "wrong number of fields" rejection.
- `hello_extra_field.txt` — too many fields, drives the same
  validator from the opposite side.

INFO response seeds — drive `parse_info_response` and
`parse_replica_info_from_master`:
- `info_master_replicas.txt` — well-formed master view with two
  connected replicas (10.0.0.10:6379, 10.0.0.11:6380) and
  monotonic offsets.
- `info_replica_missing_ip_or_port.txt` — second replica missing
  required fields, exercises the partial-record skip path.
- `info_high_offsets.txt` — large repl offsets to keep the int
  parsing path on its 64-bit branch.
- `info_slave_up.txt` / `info_slave_down.txt` — replica-side
  views with `master_link_status` flipping on/off and
  `master_link_down_since` populated for the down case.
- `info_slave_bad_numbers.txt` — slave-side INFO with malformed
  numerics so the role/link/since fields each take their
  None-on-error branch.
- `info_noise_colons.txt` — extra `:` characters and stray
  blank lines so the "ignore unknown line" path stays
  reachable from a corpus seed.

Contract test (`crates/fr-sentinel/src/lib.rs`):

Add `fuzz_sentinel_parsers_corpus_matches_documented_contract`,
which:
- Walks every file in the corpus root and runs each seed
  through `HelloMessage::parse`, `parse_replica_info_from_master`,
  and `parse_info_response` so every parser stays panic-free
  under the entire corpus.
- Pins concrete oracle expectations for the named seeds:
  - `hello_valid_ipv4.txt` parses to (sentinel_port=26379,
    master_port=6379).
  - `hello_invalid_port.txt` and `hello_extra_field.txt` both
    return `None`.
  - `info_master_replicas.txt` produces exactly two
    `(ip, port, slave_repl_offset)` triples in the documented
    order.
  - `info_replica_missing_ip_or_port.txt` yields a single
    survivor at (10.0.0.12, 6382).
  - `info_slave_down.txt` drives `Role::Slave`, link status
    `false`, and `master_link_down_since=42_000`.
  - `info_noise_colons.txt` still resolves to a master with
    `connected_slaves=Some(0)` despite the noise lines.
- Picks up new corpus seeds automatically through the directory
  walk, so future seed additions keep the contract honest
  without test edits.

`crates/fr-sentinel/src/health.rs`: incidental rustfmt fix to
the `use` ordering pulled in alongside the new test module —
no behavior change.

Co-Authored-By: Claude <noreply@anthropic.com>
